reasoning: MC dó requires OC with lˤ- > d-, ˤǝ > ō/ó and final ʔ > acute tone: lˤǝʔ → dó.
OC pˤǝk maps with ˤǝ → õ and final k retained: pˤǝk → põk.
Filling the remaining MC slot; the OC entry here must yield an MC -ãt form.
MC bãk/bák corresponds to OC -ˤak; thus the missing OC form is bˤak.
OC dzˤǝ (no final ʔ or s) gives MC with ō: dzō.
OC lˤaj loses -j and lˤ- > d-, yielding level tone ā: dā.
MC sī regularly reflects OC ǝ without ˤ: sǝ → sī.
pˤajʔs has final -s → grave tone: pà.
pˤajʔ has final ʔ → acute tone: pá.
pajʔ (no ˤ) with ʔ and -aj → pjé.
pe → palatalized j before e: pjē.
pˤek keeps -k; ˤe gives õ/ẽ before -k: pẽk.
mraj → r becomes j after consonant: mjē.
kraj: medial r → j; -aj → -ē in open syllables.
nˤǝ: ˤǝ → ō; no final gives level tone.
rak: initial r → l; -ak → -jẽk in MC.
pre: r → j after p; e → ē.
bˤǝʔ: ˤǝ → ó and final ʔ → acute tone.
mˤajs: final -s → grave tone; -aj → -a.
ŋjē points to an OC r-medial before -aj (→ jē): ŋraj.
tõk reflects ˤǝ with final -k retained: tˤǝk.

answer: Final ʔ > rising tone (acute); final s > falling/low tone (grave); open syllables keep level tone (macron). OC -p/-t/-k are retained in MC. OC ǝ > i, but ǝ with pharyngealization (ˤ) > o. OC e > ē (and before -p/-k often written ê/ẽ). Sequence -aj loses -j: → ā in plain syllables, but with r-medial it becomes -jē/-jé depending on tone. R-medial becomes palatal glide j after the initial; initial r- becomes l-. Pharyngealized lˤ- merges to MC d-. Pharyngealization is otherwise lost but colors the vowel.
reasoning: Summary of systematic correspondences visible across the table.
